mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor force and consumer income, driving corporate earnings and stock prices higher. The relationship is monotonic over the 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If rolling 3-year Rank IC of PAYEMS change vs 20-day forward returns is below 0.05, or if positive PAYEMS surprises in the top quartile do not correspond to above-median returns over 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% triggers recession fears, reducing risk appetite and lowering equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If after unemployment crosses 5%, SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances, or if the average return does not differ from non-triggered periods at the 5% significance level.

mechanism_narrative: When the real federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS minus CPI inflation) is negative, accommodative monetary policy boosts asset prices as investors seek yield, leading to positive forward returns.
failure_condition: If during episodes of negative real rates, SPX 20-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) or if the signal's IC is insignificant over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A confirmed increase in real GDP over two consecutive quarters signals that the economy is exiting a recession, leading to a sustained rally in equities over the following 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth positive for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: If after two quarters of GDP growth, SPX 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40% of cases or if average annualized return is below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1% signals a contraction in manufacturing, prompting downward earnings revisions and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: If after a 1% INDPRO decline, SPX 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of cases, or if the average 5-day return is not significantly negative (t-test p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate robust consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and investor confidence, leading to higher stock prices over the next 20 days.
failure_condition: If the monthly change in RSAFS has a Rank IC below 0.03 against 20-day forward returns over a 3-year rolling window, or if positive changes do not lead to above-median returns in 60% of months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive growth in payrolls and retail sales confirms a self-reinforcing cycle of employment and consumption, strongly boosting equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Both PAYEMS and RSAFS growing > 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: If when both signals are positive, SPX 20-day returns are below the median unconditional return in more than 50% of cases, or if the joint signal's IC is not significantly different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts forecasts a weakening in residential investment and consumer wealth effect, leading to negative equity returns over the longer 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If after a 5% drop in HOUST, SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances or if the average return is not significantly negative (t-test p < 0.10).
